title: The Colony That Vanished
brief: In 1587, 115 English settlers vanished from Roanoke Island without a trace. The only clue they left behind was the word 'CROATOAN' carved into a wooden post.

title: The Dancing Plague of 1518
brief: In 1518, hundreds of Strasbourg citizens suddenly began dancing uncontrollably for days and weeks. Many died of exhaustion, strokes, or heart attacks.

title: The Poisoning of the Green
topic: The toxic arsenic green wallpapers of Victorian England
brief: In Victorian England, a vibrant pigment called Scheele's Green became incredibly fashionable. Little did they know, it was packed with deadly arsenic, slowly poisoning entire families in their own homes.

title: The Ships in the Ice
topic: Sir John Franklin's lost Arctic expedition of 1845
brief: Franklin's expedition set out to find the Northwest Passage with two state-of-the-art ships, HMS Erebus and HMS Terror. They got trapped in the ice, and all 129 men vanished into the frozen wilderness.

title: The Aztec Sound of Death
topic: The Aztec Death Whistle history and horror
brief: Unearthing the terrifying skull-shaped whistles found in Aztec temples. When blown, they make a sound described as 'a thousand screaming corpses.'

title: The 19th Century Vampires
brief: In the 1800s, consumption (tuberculosis) ravaged New England. Desperate townspeople believed the deceased were rising from their graves to drain the living, leading to gruesome body exhumations.

title: The Library of Dust
topic: The secrets of the Vatican Secret Archives
brief: A journey into the most guarded archives in the world, containing centuries of secrets, trials of heretics, and transcripts of exorcisms.

title: The Devil’s Footprints
brief: After a heavy snowfall in Devon, England, a single line of mysterious cloven hoof-like prints appeared overnight, stretching for 100 miles, crossing high walls and rooftops.

title: The Death of the Explorer
topic: The mysterious death of Meriwether Lewis in 1809
brief: The co-leader of the Lewis and Clark expedition died of gunshot wounds in a remote cabin. Officially ruled a suicide, strange details suggest it was cold-blooded murder.

title: The Poison Cup
topic: The toxic makeup of Queen Elizabeth I
brief: Queen Elizabeth I covered her face in Venetian Ceruse, a cosmetics mixture made of toxic white lead and vinegar, slowly corroding her skin and poisoning her body over decades.
